maritime.title: NERAI Maritime Weekly
events: US-Iran War Drives Hormuz Transits to Near-Standstill; Two Tankers Attacked
Houthis Resume Attacks on Saudi Tankers, Threaten Bab el-Mandeb Blockade
Gulf Producers Race to Activate Hormuz Bypass Routes as Oil Tops $96
Somali Pirates Seize Tanker in Gulf of Aden Amid Regional Security Vacuum
Iran Strikes US Bases in Kuwait and Bahrain; Trump Threatens Infrastructure Strikes
maritime.summary: This week's maritime landscape was dominated by the escalating US-Iran conflict and its cascading effects on the two most critical energy chokepoints — the Strait of Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb. Iran conducted direct attacks on commercial vessels near Hormuz while the Houthis simultaneously resumed strikes on Saudi oil tankers and threatened a full blockade of the Red Sea, forcing multiple vessels to reverse course. The dual-chokepoint pressure has pushed oil prices above $96, rattled global supply chains, and is forcing Asian refiners and Gulf producers to urgently seek alternative routing and pipeline infrastructure.

sanctions.title: NERAI Sanctions Weekly
events: U.S. Treasury Doubles Down on Iran: Shamkhani Shipping Empire & Weapons Network Hit
EU Russia Sanctions Package Stalls; Oil Price Cap Locked In Amid Member-State Fractures
U.S. Cuba Sanctions Escalation Forces Meliá Exit and Draws Espionage Charges
OFAC VPN Sanctions Produce Global Telegram Short-Link Outage — Collateral Digital Infrastructure Risk
South Korea Seizes Vessel in North Korea UN Sanctions Violation; Putin-DPRK Diplomat Meeting Signals Axis Deepening
sanctions.summary: This week's sanctions landscape was dominated by sustained U.S. pressure on Iran's illicit networks and shipping empire, a fracturing EU consensus on Russia that allowed fossil-fuel revenues to remain robust, and an escalating Cuba crackdown with tangible third-party corporate casualties. Secondary signals — a Danish shipyard controversy, a North Korea vessel seizure, and collateral digital infrastructure damage from a VPN designation — underscore that enforcement tools are increasingly producing unintended systemic consequences alongside deliberate strategic pressure.
